Text: Do artificial earthquakes really exist, yun?
There used to be internet jokes about certain universities doing nuclear experiments underground.
If the news is true, then Q-chan's conspiracy theories just made the world a little more peaceful, yun!
I'd be happy if it means fewer anime slots getting cancelled for breaking news.
I don't care what their reasons are. All scalpers should be executed.
Q-chan, you're starting to sound like an execution angel, yun!
How many times have those bastards screwed me over...!!
An otaku's grudge is terrifying, yun.
Looks like the anti-scalper people are joining the 'bad guys' just to crush the scalpers, yun.
There are definitely hopeless people out there who'll use any means necessary.
Games, Anime, Figures, and Idols. It's about content industries with global influence.
Some online video ads really stick with you, don't they? They get stuck in your head.
Wait! Maybe they're not after computer viruses but meme infection, yun!
Like how pink-haired heroines are trouble, or blue-haired ones always lose...?
Purple hair definitely means little devil type, yun.
It's true! The more people there are, the more we advance yun!
Chicken and the egg. Could be that civilization advancing is what lets the population grow.
If the population gets 10 million times bigger, our tech will advance so much that humanity will spread across the galaxy yun!
Well yeah, Earth wouldn't be big enough anymore.
Oh right. If the needles are silver, they'd actually hurt vampires.
Werewolves would get caught in the crossfire too yun.
Fantasy, tearjerkers, moe, cat ears! Vampire stories are great no matter what genre they're in.
I think one of those doesn't quite fit with the others yun.
Wait! If I became a vampire, that'd be the perfect excuse to stay inside and finally get through my game backlog and all that anime!
Vampires still have to go outside to find blood, yun.
Do you remember your first love, Q-chan?
My first love is you, Yunyun!
Mom likes villain-type characters and mischievous little princesses.
Hehe! I'm a villain and a little demon, yun!
I learned this from a PC game, but apparently there was some religion that said all misfortune comes from the curse of first love.
If Q-chan's first love is Yunyun, maybe this whole situation is some kind of curse too, yun?
This! Is! Absolutely! True!! If you actually look into it, you'll see the proof!
My Q-chan is so smart, yun.
I repeat moe songs ten thousand times a day with pure gratitude.
Every time I hear moe words in denpa songs, I feel like my soul gets purified.
True gamers would probably grind RTAs even in hell.
...Death matches in Naraka hell with Sisyphus' rock... That actually sounds like a fun challenge, yun?
What kind of games do you like, Q-chan?
Dating sims, rhythm games, parry games. I like games where you get that satisfying button press timing.
'That Time I Set the Fastest RTA Record at Sai no Kawara River and Got Reincarnated as a Genius Pro Gamer Dominating the Real World.'
Sounds like something from a web novel or webtoon, yun.
Just adding the word "late-night" makes it feel all secretive and underground, yun.
Having to sneak and turn on the TV without Mom noticing was already pretty dokidoki.
When I think of late-night anime sponsors, I picture music labels and merchandise companies, yun.
Hymns and idol worship... looks like it has all the elements religion needs.
If late-night anime is demon worship, does that make fictional characters demons?
In the sense that they're imaginary beings that tempt people and steal their free time... their lifespan... maybe they're the same, yun?
You see theories about humanity having cosmic origins all the time. Alien stuff and cyberpunk RPGs.
Creators are usually the root of all evil, yun.
In fiction, marriage feels like this unknown world that comes after the credits roll, yun.
True, not many works actually show marriage. It only happens in sequels or fan fiction.
If marriage is punishment, you probably can't just divorce on a whim either.
The whole "I'll never let you go" thing, yun.
I don't really get philosophical zombies, but parents are amazing. They can sacrifice so much for their kids.
Not just genetics either. When you think about how much parents influence personality and behavior, it's huge.
I wonder if kids are so precious to parents that they'd give up everything for them?
Do you ever go outside, Q-chan?
Haha. They're called miracles because they don't happen.
Even if this conspiracy is real, it doesn't matter to a nocturnal hikikomori like you, yun!
It might get packed with night owls, yun.
Come on, normies. Throw away your common sense and face me!
Did Q-chan's school have any ghost stories, yun?
Who knows. I did encounter the mysterious phenomenon of my indoor shoes going missing a lot in middle school.
I don't really get what 'loner cosplay' means, yun.
No matter how much they cosplay, they can't put on the real aura of someone with a wounded heart.
Q-chan, when bad things happened to you at school, did you ever hate the people who didn't help you, yun?
It was too painful. I didn't have the energy to hate or resent anyone.
What do you think is the right way to handle comment wars on social media, yun?
Comment wars aren't about winning. They're about not losing.
The internet's full of fights again today, yun.
This world is 50.78% anger, 44.2% hate, and the remaining 4.493% is Yunyun.
Even on the day the evil god was supposed to descend, nothing happened, yun!
Ahhh. Guess there really is no god after all.
Stories that never get finished make me feel so lonely, yun.
The people in fictional worlds are so pitiful.
If you could read the continuation of any canceled series, which one would you want to read, yun?
If it's not just manga, there's anime that ended after one season, novels that never got their third volume... too many to choose from.
If you could read more of 'Execution Angel † Guiltina,' would you be happy, yun?
Part of me wants to see Yunyun in action again, but... it ended so well that a forced sequel might be complicated.
What does AND YOU mean, yun?
It's like a thank you for playing kind of thing.
Do you watch the credits all the way to the end, yun?
Of course. I like to savor the afterglow, whether it's games or movies.
Game credits sometimes have so many people listed, yun.
Games are like total art forms where you build entire worlds, so I guess it takes that many people.
Q-chan, don't you ever do live streams or anything yun?
I became a hikikomori because I hate people and society. There's no way I could do something that terrifying.
Q-chan, don't you want to get money from denpa receivers like with Super Chats yun?
I'm happy just being able to broadcast my love for Yunyun.
Live streams and game streaming are really popular these days yun.
Yeah. But I'm not interested in anything besides Yunyun.
It's a microchip they implant inside your body.
Even if cats' cuteness is actually dark and twisted, I still wouldn't be able to resist petting them, yun!
That's what you call spoiling a cat!
Cats are mysterious. Something that cute has to be packed with cosmic secrets, yun!
I heard there's a mystical equation where infinity minus a maiden's heart equals cat ears.
Q-chan, do you think summers are getting hotter and hotter yun?
I wonder? Hard to say. Mom has been getting mad about the electric bill getting more expensive though.
What do you think of when you think of summer, Q-chan yun?
Machine guns! Or maybe... Summer! The beach! School swimsuits!
Human memory is so unreliable... Hey, Q-chan, do you have any memories from last summer, yun?
I'm the kind of person who can't even keep track of what day it is, let alone seasons.
When Q-chan's in dokidoki mode, she never loses focus, yun.
Nothing can interfere with love's denpa!
If parallel worlds exist, which world would you want to go to, yun?
Maybe one where Mom actually loves me more?
If this world started five minutes ago, would our feelings of 'love' be fake too, yun?
I like what I like, so deal with it!! Whether those memories are fake or real, my love stays the same!
Come to think of it, you and I never made any kind of contract, but that's okay?
A contract is formed when there's mutual agreement, yun. The fact that you and I are connected through denpa and spending time together is the answer, yun.
Demons... fictional beings are immortal as long as people remember them, yun.
Thanatophobia, right? Thinking about disappearing someday is pretty scary.
Yunyun will be with Q-chan's soul forever, yun. So you'll never be alone, yun. It's okay, yun.
Come to think of it, birds descended from dinosaurs, yun.
Really? So instead of 'chicken or the egg,' it's more like 'chicken or dinosaur.'
Like finding a random 100 yen coin in your pocket that you don't remember putting there, yun?
All kinds of viruses are in the news, yun! Q-chan, take care of your health, yun!
It's a closed world, so I'm fine!
Whoa. Making clouds from people's sweat is amazing, yun!
Apparently it could be defined as a real cloud meteorologically speaking too.
I've heard there are more paranormal phenomena near water, yun!
There was a French scientist who proposed a theory that water remembers information from substances that were once dissolved in it.
If it's so hot that clouds are forming, why do people still go to event venues, yun?
'You can't stop the longing!' That's why!
Oh, Yunyun has a Wiki page too, yun.
Hehehe. I have a wonderful proposal... Why don't you become a Yunyun fan too!
Following Wikipedia links one after another is so exciting, yun.
It's a dead term now, but internet surfing is fun.
When I see censored words, it reminds me of those 'words you shouldn't search' lists, yun.
Be careful searching. You might end up with the same ads everywhere.
There's that saying about being born under a certain star... maybe satellites count as stars too, yun.
Makes sense. Maybe that's why there are so many internet addicts since satellites went up.
If the latest astrology theories are true, satellite positions could change your destiny, yun!
...Could a satellite be the reason my life turned out like this?!
If the satellite is broken, it affects kids born under that star too, yun.
I see. So at least one of them must be seriously broken.
Q-chan, your body is basically made of instant food and energy drinks, yun?
Then I'll never need to cook in my lifetime. I'll crush everything with unlimited processed food!
What's your favorite instant food, Q-chan, yun?
Wait... could the reason I can talk to Yunyun be because of instant food?!
No way, yun! It's because of our love denpa, yun!
What's an internet fairy from the old days, yun?
Do those old personal blogs still exist somewhere, yun?
...The internet's last bit of decency. Those static HTML sites...
...For some reason, those electronic fairy girls apparently have ripped abs, yun...!
I see. Maybe their ID numbers were really big.
Q-chan has a bunch of figures in her room too, yun!
Digital's fine, but as an otaku, you want to give form to what you love.
The first day I displayed a Yunyun figure at home, I felt like I'd gotten closer to five-star otaku status.
With or without figures, if you love Yunyun, you're five-star, yun!
Maybe I can talk to Yunyun like this because I performed some evil god summoning ritual with my figures?
Yunyun's not an evil god, she's a demon, yun. Besides, Yunyun and Q-chan's connection comes from the miracle of denpa!
If all the stars are fake, then the chance of meeting aliens is 0%, yun!
That's reassuring. No pure love turning Earth into a meat blob, or getting attacked by hostile alien life forms.
If the whole sky is a planetarium, I can have unlimited shooting star substitutes, yun!
Otaku have this image of loving Morse code, yun!
I don't want to be like everyone else, but I don't want no one to get me either. I like words that only certain people understand.
Speaking of which Q-chan, aren't you interested in Vtubers? There's lots of cute people, yun!
I like them, but not as much as you do. Vtubers are just documentaries wrapped in 2D.
Video platforms keep popping up one after another, yun.
Too many makes me tired from researching and choosing. I miss when Nitanita Video (β) dominated everything.
Lately the word 'reincarnation' seems to be trending everywhere, yun.
I don't know who came up with that, but talk about half-assed.
Agent Parrot's world sounds pretty brutal, yun.
Even with animal protection laws, labor standards don't apply to them.
There are quite a few apps with bird icons, yun.
Like that pushy language learning app too.
Ugh. Even just hearing the word Monday makes my skin crawl.
God must have had Monday phobia too, yun.
Hikikomori NEETs don't get any rest. We're always suffering on a bed of nails made of guilt.
Q-chan is working hard, yun! Just living and being happy makes you five stars, yun!
If this conspiracy theory gets us Mondays off, everyone's gonna be happy, yun!
True! Wait... but... what day of the week is it anyway?
Millionaires are NEETs? I guess I have heard that really rich people live quietly.
...Could it be... Q-chan, are you secretly...?
Sigh. Even if we're both NEETs, we're completely different.
Q-chan is one of a kind and special to Yunyun, so that's only natural, yun!
Back at our old place, Dad had paid for satellite TV, so I could watch all kinds of anime on the premium channels.
Watching anime marathons is like a little festival, yun!
I've even made some overseas otaku friends because we bonded over Yunyun.
The denpa of love crosses all borders, yun!
It's painful when you can't watch your favorite shows because of all these restrictions, yun.
Anime, games, novels... I wish everyone could enjoy them more easily, regardless of their situation.
Does Q-chan use digital money too, yun?
I use something called BITMOEE. It's convenient for buying downloadable games.
What does Q-chan do when there's something you want, yun?
I gradually use up the allowance and New Year's money I've been saving.
Otaku denpa drives the economy, yun!
Hearing that makes me a little proud as a fellow otaku.
'Execution Angel † Guiltina' is aimed at guys, so how come you like it, Q-chan, yun?
Because you're so moe, Yunyun! When it comes to moe, it doesn't matter if it's for guys, girls, HL, GL, or BL!
If we lived in a world where people could make their ideal kids, I probably wouldn't have been born.
Then Q-chan's miraculous existence is totally five-star, yun!
I can't picture myself ever becoming someone's parent down the line.
Q-chan's life belongs to Q-chan, yun. As long as Q-chan's happy, nothing else matters, yun.
Q-chan, do you play gacha games, yun?
I don't, but if a Yunyun game came out I would. I'd throw my entire fortune at it to show my love for Yunyun!
Denpa songs. Gacha. MAD videos. The digital world is full of electronic drugs, yun.
But for me, the best electronic drug is Yunyun's voice!
Is dumbing down the masses really such a bad thing? I think being stupid lets you live happier with a smile.
Control and submission, beautifully. Give and take, yun.
It's a wild conspiracy theory, but I've seen this stuff online before. There was also that thing about Finland not existing.
Huh? The internet's got a lot of philosophers, yun.
There are plenty of animals that don't seem like they could be real. Like that beaver that goes 'AHHHHH.'
That's a creature that only exists on the internet, yun.
Q-chan, do you have a favorite cryptid, yun?
Some otakus just can't hold a conversation, you know? Like, you can't get through to them at all.
It's not like they're playing catch with words... more like dodgeball, yun.
I'm not S or M... I'm Y! Y for Yunyun!
Fufu. Then Yunyun is Q派, yun!
If I copy the way Yunyun talks, will I become a cute, angelic girl too, yun?
Yunyun's more of a little devil, yun.
Are you okay with scary stories, Q-chan, yun?
I'm fine with them, I guess? I went through a phase where I'd read creepypasta for like 22 hours a day.
This is embarrassing, but I used to believe everything I read online and even tried summoning demons.
Pure Q-chan believing in demons is so cute and wonderful, yun!
I heard that if you tell scary stories, real ghosts actually show up, yun!
I was thinking about Yunyun so much that she appeared... Maybe there's something to it?
If there's a theory that the world is made of pasta, then maybe a religion where Yunyun created the world isn't so crazy?
A world where PC game characters can become gods is totally messed up, yun.
Speaking of which, I heard the code for "Execution Angel † Guiltina" is complete spaghetti code.
If it works, it's good enough, yun!
When I watch space science videos or read articles, I get totally overwhelmed by how vast and amazing the universe is.
But all that amazing stuff about the universe only matters because Q-chan is there to appreciate it, yun!
If we use the DHMO joke as a reference, even Yunyun could be made to sound dangerous.
...The 1000-year survival rate of people who have known 'Yunyun' is 0%, yun...!
Q-chan, don't you drink alcohol yun?
Hehe. I don't need alcohol when I've got Yunyun!
Eat what you want, drink what you want, have fun! A stress-free life is the healthiest yun!
As long as I have Yunyun, I'm good.
It is kind of weird how birds always perch on power lines like that yun.
I feel like I haven't seen that in a while, which is also weird.
Maybe Mom's been mad about the electric bill lately because bird drones are stealing our power?
We found the real culprits yun!
As technology keeps advancing, you never know where, when, or how someone might be watching you, yun.
For someone like you, I propose the ultimate personal information protection: being a hikikomori!
Exploring the unknown gets your heart racing. Reminds me of my first night diving into the internet.
What got you into games and the internet, yun?
Dad left his computer and games behind. I got curious and started messing around with them... next thing I knew, I was deep in the otaku wasteland.
Stories set in the Arctic always have dangerous alien creatures showing up. Staying holed up in my room is definitely the safest option.
In Q-chan's case, it's more like 'Denpa Entity Y from Delusions,' yun.

So I say: Yunyun is me. And I'm allowed to love myself. No reason needed. Even if the world doesn't allow it, Yunyun allowed it. If Yunyun allowed it, that's enough. Every morning when I get out of bed, I remember: I'm allowed to exist in this world. I'm allowed to like myself. I'm allowed to love myself! When I first saw Yunyun, I had an existential crisis. What if Yunyun was the real me and this me was fake? What if Yunyun noticed my existence? What if Yunyun herself awakened to her own denpa? But now I understand. Yunyun is me, and I am me. And I'm allowed to love myself. No one can deny this anymore. Because I have received Yunyun's denpa.

Q-chan is an anonymous poster who suddenly appeared on the internet. She continuously posts incomprehensible and fragmentary "strange documents," and numerous interpreters and believers have emerged around them.

Q-chan's strange documents were initially regarded as mere internet memes, but due to several events described below, they have social influence.

Q-chan is an anonymous poster who suddenly appeared online. They continuously post incomprehensible and fragmentary "strange writings," around which many interpreters and believers have emerged.

Q-chan's strange writings were initially regarded as mere internet memes, but due to several events described below, they have gained social influence and are considered the central element of the phenomenon called Yunyun Denpa Syndrome.

Text is available under the Creative Commons Attribution-ShareAlike License.
Privacy Policy

Q-chan became widely known when shitposts about the character "Yunyun" from 'Execution Angel † Guiltina' became a topic on social media and spread. The incomprehensible texts drew attention from some as "interesting" and "addictive."

Simultaneously with the spread of Q-chan's shitposts, a group emerged that interpreted the texts as codes and prophecies. They were called "Q-chan Denpa Receivers" and contributed to the further spread and increased influence of Q-chan's shitposts.

The social attention given to Q-chan's shitposts and Q-chan Denpa Receivers began with an incident where she supposedly predicted an earthquake. This became a major topic mainly in occult and pseudoscience communities, and from this incident, Q-chan began to be treated as a prophetic figure on the internet.
Q-chan's shitposts contain no conspiratorial thoughts or claims. They remain limited to expressions of deep (?) love for "Yunyun."

The claims of Q-chan Denpa Receivers are extremely diverse, with no unified view. Their interpretations vary, but they are consistent in that they are uniformly treated by third parties as "conspiracy theories."
The released photo was spread as "Denpa Delusional Beauty Q" and supposedly expanded the support base in a different context than before.

Regarding Q-chan's true identity, theories such as "single person theory," "bot theory," and "government organization operation theory" have been proposed, but there is no decisive evidence, and all remain within the realm of speculation.

Cases have been reported of cognitive distortions such as "hallucinations," "intensification of obsessive thoughts," and "decreased ability to distinguish between dreams and reality" occurring in Q-chan Denpa Receivers and people who read Q-chan's shitposts for extended periods.
Continued exposure to Q-chan and related strange writings and conspiracy theories can cause cognitive distortion in some cases, and this condition is called Yunyun Denpa Syndrome.
P-chan is another account or alias that posts bizarre documents similar to Q-chan's posts.

Attention was drawn after claims that "P-chan was posting about Yunyun before Q-chan" and discovery of past log timestamps. However, there are also opinions pointing out the possibility of timestamp tampering, so the truth is uncertain.

An incident where riots allegedly occurred around the Tokyo Metropolitan Government building as a result of P-chan's bizarre documents and conspiracy theories spreading (commonly known as: Tokyo Metropolitan Government Riot Incident). Since this incident, the Q-chan account has stopped updating, and no new posts have been confirmed for about 9 months.

An incident where riots allegedly occurred around the Tokyo Metropolitan Government building as a result of P-chan's bizarre documents and conspiracy theories spreading (commonly known as: Tokyo Metropolitan Government Riot Incident).

It has been confirmed that P-chan is an AI trained on Q-chan's posts as its learning data.
This system is said to have been built by a development team that had detected Yunyun Denpa Syndrome at an early stage, for the purpose of exploiting Yunyun Denpa Syndrome.
"Yunyun Denpa Syndrome" is a cognitive abnormality that people who have been continuously exposed to Q-chan's bizarre documents or Q denpa receivers' conspiracy theories are said to develop.

・Hallucinations
・Intensified obsessive thoughts
・Diminished ability to distinguish dreams from reality
・Conspiratorial thinking

The National Center of Neurology and Psychiatry and an advisory committee of experts have also reported, based on analysis of social media browsing and posting history as well as questionnaire surveys, that users with higher frequency of contact with related content show a greater tendency to develop Yunyun Denpa Syndrome.

"Execution Angel † Guiltina" is a PC game with visual novel and simulation elements released in 2003 by indie brand "WHO YOU." The genre is "false conviction execution-type apocalypse RPG." Yunyun is a little devil from the enemy organization that appears in the work. Her first person pronoun is "Yunyun." Her verbal tic is "yun."

A coined term from the work. With the release of the 2005 "Execution Angel † Guiltina" OVA, the phrase "Rim de Lacent!" became a topic on the internet, mainly among anime fans on 666ch. It won the silver prize at the 2005 "Anime Buzzword Awards." The OVA director explained it as a phrase meaning "let's go to 2D" and "it's okay to escape from reality."

The author has gone on record that he firmly believes in the philosophy that being an idiot is easier. This influenced his decision to have Yunyun's catchphrase be a seemingly meaningless jumble of words.

Yunyun is a fictional character who appears in the multimedia work "Execution Angel † Guiltina." She is depicted as a "denpa little devil" who enjoys high popularity despite being an antagonist in the story. Originally from the PC game released in 2003, she later appeared in various media expansions including the 2005 OVA adaptation and character song CD releases.

Guiltina's line common to all endings. In every ending, quiet townscapes are always shown at the end, and as the BGM fades out, she says "The world is clean." This also serves as the cue for the staff roll, after which the ED "Vibraslap of Victory" plays.

This work was supported not for its high strategic nature, but for the exhilarating feeling of being able to execute scumbags and scumbag-like beings with comical, well-paced splatter expression. Since it doesn't require difficult decisions, it's sometimes mockingly called a "muscle training puzzle you can play with reflexes alone." The flashy execution effects and highly decisive Executes enabled speedy gameplay, making it a hot topic as a work suited for stress relief.
→ For details, see "<click=Chara>Execution Angel † Guiltina Character List</click>"

The protagonist of this work. High-ranking Execution Angel. Possesses reflexes and reaction speed that allow her hands to move before thinking, boasting the highest number of executions in Heaven. She has almost no hesitation about executions, and due to her attitude of prioritizing efficiency and speed above all else, she is treated as an "elite" within Heaven.

A Denpa little devil. After the OVA adaptation, her "Rim de Lacent" catchphrase and character song became internet memes.
→See details: <click=YunYun>Yunyun</click>

The OVA version of 'Execution Angel † Guiltina' released in 2005 in 3 volumes was based on the original game's story while emphasizing comedic parts and simplifying some depictions.
The splatter expressions in particular were turned into gags for the OVA, and the direction of Guiltina's execution scenes became a topic of discussion.

Due to its extreme expressions and clear-cut values, opinions were greatly divided at the time of release, but it later became known as a work that gained cult popularity.
Yunyun in particular became memeified on the internet and is treated as a mascot character symbolizing this work.

The game was built around the concept that "Scumbags must be punished," so the characters themselves delight in dishing out that punishment. "Execution Angels" wreak havoc throughout human society, massacring those deemed "scumbags" with brutal, splatter-inducing ferocity. The series is known not only for its gore, but also its thought-provoking story that artfully uses satire and moments of comic relief to poke at modern day issues.

The Execution Angel † Guiltina OVA was released in 2005 as a six volume set.

In Heaven, Death is deemed the ultimate salvation, the only salvation for those deemed scumbags. Execution Angels take it upon themselves to cleanse the human world of corruptive scum of the earth. In response, Demons attempt to stand up against this false justice under the banner that even societal scum can be saved. They grant the Angels' targets true love, money, power and information in an attempt to give them a second chance at life. However, the scum abuse their gifts, only accelerating their own demise.

Among all of the Execution Angels in Heaven, Guiltina is by far the strongest, boasting the highest execution rate of them all. An elite warrior, she has instantaneous reflexes, allowing her to move in battle without even thinking. On the other end of the spectrum is Eve. While technically an Execution Angel, she somehow still has her memories from when she was human. Unable to stand the sight of blood, she joined the ranks of the Demons to try and staunch the slaughter.

After countless executions, when every single corrupted scumbag was gone, Guiltina raised her blood soaked hands to the sky and proclaimed, "The world is cleansed." All is quiet, the world at peace, but in that moment, Guiltina feels the emptiness that comes along with a mission fulfilled.
A divine being with the sole purpose of cleansing human society of those they deem "scum of the Earth." Every Execution Angel was once themselves a miserable scumbag with a human soul.
Hunted by the Execution Angels and protected by the Demons, they are deemed to be a scourge on human society. Most of them are egotistical bastards that are loyal only to their base desires.

Acting under the belief that "even scumbags can be saved," they interfere in human affairs. They grant powers related to desires such as love, money, power, and information, but as a result often hasten the self-destruction of scumbags.
An RPG that calls itself a "False Accusation Execution-Type Apocalypse RPG." The player acts as an Execution Angel who executes "scumbags" in the human world on sight to make the world "clean."

Scumbag judgment is vague, and the determination is arbitrarily distorted by the player's subjectivity. "Whether you executed or not" becomes the progression condition rather than right or wrong, and the world normally moves forward even with false accusations.

If an Execution Angel goes a certain number of turns without executing, their violence impulse gauge builds up, and when it exceeds the threshold, they go berserk. During a rampage, they attack without distinguishing between enemies and allies.
Execution Angel † Guiltina is a PC game developed by indie studio "WHO YOU" released in 2003. The devs have described its genre as, "an apocalyptic RPG where the innocent face execution."
